lesson.menu_title: Saying Who You Are -- Present Simple for Identity and Role
lesson.menu_desc: Say what you do, what you manage, and who you are -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- Saying Who You Are -- Present Simple for Identity, Role, and Institutional Life
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 2 -- Present Simple for Identity and Role

listenings.text: Good evening, everyone, and welcome to our university in Seoul. My name is Grace, and I work in the international office. Tonight, we welcome guests from many countries. Our program is simple. First, we have a short welcome. Then, our rector says a few words. After that, we have dinner together in the main hall. Please feel at home. If you need help, our team is here for you. Thank you, and enjoy your evening.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Daniel from the conference office. I have some information for you. The round table starts at ten o'clock, in Room Two. You speak after the professor from Japan. Each speaker has ten minutes. Please bring your name badge to the front desk before nine thirty. If you have a question, you can call me. Thank you, and have a good day.

extra_audio.text: Hello. My name is Maria Clara. I am a professor, and I manage a team. I am responsible for international partnerships.

reading.rtitle: A Day in Maria Clara's Life
paras: Maria Clara lives in Maceio, in the northeast of Brazil. She works at a university, and she also works with a public council. Every morning, she teaches her students. In the afternoon, she manages a team of eight people. She represents her institution at important meetings.

Maria Clara wears many hats. She is a professor, a leader, and a grandmother. She travels to other countries for her work, and an interpreter usually goes with her. But this year is different. In September, she travels to China, and in November, she travels to Seoul. This time, she wants to speak English on her own.

At home, her granddaughter Clarinha is three years old. Clarinha studies a little English at school. When Maria Clara speaks English with Clarinha, she is not nervous. She is responsible for many things at work, but Clarinha is her favorite reason to learn.
reading.source: A personal profile written for this course

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Maria Clara wants to stop working and travel for fun.
Maria Clara has a busy institutional life, and this year she wants to speak English on her own.
Maria Clara only speaks English with her students at the university.

items: Maria Clara works at a university and with a public council.
The first paragraph says she works at a university and also with a public council.
In the afternoon, she manages a team of eight people.
The text states this directly in the first paragraph.
An interpreter never travels with Maria Clara.
The text says an interpreter usually goes with her on her work trips.
This year, Maria Clara wants to speak English on her own.
In September and November she travels, and this time she wants to speak English on her own.

tips: I am a professor, and I manage a team.
I + verb: I work, I manage, I teach
I work at a university in Brazil.
I am responsible for international partnerships.

items.situation: "Tell me about Clarinha. What does she do?"
tips: she + verb + s: studies, works, lives
No, my team stays in Brazil. I travel alone.

items.situation: A speaker talks very fast and you don't catch it.
tips: Sorry, I don't understand. Could you repeat, please?
Asking to repeat is control, not weakness.
